text: The electro-oxidation of alcohols to carbon dioxide with direct scission of carboncarbon (C-C) bond over catalyst remains a major challenge in achieving the technological advancement of direct alcohol fuel cells (DAFCs) for large-scale energy storage and conversion. In this study, ultrathin core/shell structure Au/Pt nanowires (NWs) composed of stepped Pt clusters on a stepped Au single-crystal nanocore (thickness 2.3 nm) were obtained. The low-coordinated surface structure catalyzed the alkaline ethanol electro-oxidation reaction that was sensitive to the atomic ratio of Au to Pt. Interestingly, the mass activity of Au1.0/Pt0.2 catalyst to ethanol electro-oxidation reaction reached up to 196.9 A/mgPt. Apart from ethanol, other primary alcohols like methanol, n-propanol and ethylene glycol were also catalytically oxidized efficiently over this Au/Pt catalyst. The occurrence of various electro-oxidation reaction intermediates have been identified using operando X-ray absorption spectroscopy (XAS) and in situ infrared reflection-absorption spectroscopy (IRRAS). The stretched lattice of Pt as well as the inherent structure of the stepped surface effectively hindered the strong poisoning of catalyst by CO*, while enhanced the adsorption of the active OH* species in the alkaline solution for catalyzing the ethanol electro-oxidation efficiently. The energy barriers of C-C bond scission and desorption of CO* are reduced on under-coordinated Au-Pt facets, which was simulated by the density functional theory (DFT). A strategy for the design and preparation of catalysts toward large-scale fuel cell application has been established by modulating the surface structure for the multi-electron transformations.
